skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI-powered video creation agent. Create short videos (5-120 seconds) for ads, social media, brand content, and explainers. Handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music through the Pexo API.
skill.summary: Create professional short videos using AI. Send your request and the Pexo backend handles all production work.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: All 534 static findings evaluated as false positives or expected legitimate behavior. The external_commands patterns in .md files are documentation code blocks, not active code. Shell command substitution in scripts is standard API client implementation. Network calls target pexo.ai (the service's own API). Filesystem access is limited to config (~/.pexo/) and temp files that are properly cleaned up. Env access reads PEXO_API_KEY which is required for the API. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or obfuscation. Safe to publish with MEDIUM risk warning due to the combination of script execution, network API calls, and credential access inherent to API client tools.

medium_findings.title: API Credential in Environment Variables
medium_findings.description: PEXO_API_KEY is read from environment or config file and used for API authentication. This is expected behavior for an API client, but the credential is sent with every HTTP request in the Authorization header. The mask_secret function in pexo-doctor.sh properly masks the key in diagnostic output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate API key usage. The pattern matches the expected behavior of an authenticated API client. No data exfiltration detected.

medium_findings.title: Shell Script Execution via Command Substitution
medium_findings.description: Shell scripts use $(...) command substitution extensively for API calls, file operations, and data processing. All command substitutions are standard Bash patterns for a CLI tool and none involve unsanitized user input passed to execution contexts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution is intrinsic to shell scripting for CLI tools. All uses follow standard patterns (variable assignment, function output capture) with no injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation files contain hardcoded URLs pointing to pexo.ai. These are legitimate references to the service API, project pages, and help guides. Not malicious but creates a dependency on external infrastructure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to pexo.ai (the service the skill integrates with). Clear from context that these are legitimate service references, not malicious endpoints.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access for Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill reads configuration from ~/.pexo/config, which is a hidden file in the user's home directory. This is a standard pattern for CLI tools (similar to ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.gitconfig) and is not malicious.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard config file pattern used by many CLI tools. The file location is documented in the SKILL.md and users are instructed to create it manually. No hidden data access.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance in Diagnostic Script
low_findings.description: pexo-doctor.sh checks for system dependencies (curl, jq, file) and network connectivity. This is standard diagnostic behavior and not malicious reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The diagnostic script checks for required dependencies and network connectivity. This is expected for a diagnostic tool and serves no reconnaissance purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution via Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill executes shell scripts that run system commands (curl, jq, mkdir, mv, rm). This is expected for a Bash-based CLI tool but requires trust in the scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All shell commands serve legitimate purposes for an API client CLI tool. Commands include curl for HTTP, jq for JSON processing, and file utilities.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key in HTTP Authorization Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The PEXO_API_KEY is sent as a Bearer token in HTTP Authorization headers with every API request. This is standard for REST API authentication but means the credential is in memory during script execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Standard HTTP Bearer auth pattern. The credential is properly scoped to the pexo.ai domain. No evidence of credential forwarding or exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: File Upload and Download Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uploads user files to the Pexo API and downloads video assets to local storage. Files are temporarily stored and cleaned up appropriately.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: File operations are the core functionality of a video creation tool. Upload and download patterns follow standard REST API file handling with proper cleanup.

content.user_title: Create AI Videos with Pexo Agent
content.value_statement: Creating short videos for social media, ads, or brand content requires specialized tools and creative skills. The Pexo agent handles the entire video production workflow from script to final output through a simple chat interface.
actual_capabilities: Create short AI-generated videos from text descriptions
Upload images, video clips, and audio files as reference material
Manage video projects with create, list, and status polling
Download finished videos with signed URLs and local file caching
Check credit balance and entitlement information
Run environment diagnostics to verify setup and API connectivity
limitations: Video duration limited to 5-120 seconds only
Each message to the Pexo API consumes credits from your account
Requires a PEXO_API_KEY from pexo.ai to function
Cannot create videos without uploading files when the user provides them

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creator
use_cases.description: A content creator describes a video idea in their own words. The skill relays the request to Pexo, uploads any reference files, and delivers the finished video.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Product Marketing Video
use_cases.description: A marketer uploads product photos and describes the promotion they need. The skill creates a product video with the provided assets as visual reference.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and small business owners

use_cases.title: Video Revision Workflow
use_cases.description: After receiving a video, the user asks for changes. The skill sends revision feedback to the existing project and polls until the updated video is ready.
use_cases.target_user: Anyone who needs iterative video refinement

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second product video for my new coffee brand. The video should feel warm and inviting.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple video

prompt_templates.title: Video with Uploaded Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 30-second Instagram ad for these product photos I am uploading. The style should be modern and energetic.
prompt_templates.scenario: User providing reference images for the video

prompt_templates.title: Video Revision
prompt_templates.prompt: The video looks good but please make the text overlay bigger and change the background music to something more upbeat.
prompt_templates.scenario: User requesting changes to an existing video project

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Format Video Production
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three versions of this video: one 9:16 for TikTok, one 16:9 for YouTube, and one 1:1 for Instagram. Keep it under 20 seconds each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating multiple aspect ratios

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second product video for my new coffee brand.
output_examples.output: A confirmation message with project ID, estimated wait time of 15-20 minutes, and a project link to pexo.ai. Then after polling completes, a video file is delivered with the download URL for a 15-second coffee brand video.

output_examples.input: Make the text bigger and change the music to jazz.
output_examples.output: The request is submitted to the existing project. After processing, the revised video is delivered with updated text overlays and jazz background music.

best_practices: Describe your video idea in natural language and let Pexo handle the creative details for best results.
Upload reference images before describing your video so the AI can use them as visual context.
Check your credit balance before starting a large project to avoid interruptions.
anti_patterns: Do not add creative directions like duration, style, or music choices yourself. Pexo handles these automatically.
Do not wrap video download URLs in markdown link syntax. Send them as plain text with all query parameters.
Do not send multiple separate messages. Consolidate all requirements into one message to save credits.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a PEXO_API_KEY from pexo.ai and the required runtime tools: curl, jq, and file. The skill reads your API key from ~/.pexo/config.

faq.question: How long does it take to create a video?
faq.answer: Short videos (15 seconds) take about 15-20 minutes. Complex or longer videos may take more time.

faq.question: What video formats and sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Videos are 5-120 seconds long. Supported aspect ratios are 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1.

faq.question: Can I upload my own images and audio?
faq.answer: Yes. You can upload images (jpg, png, webp, and others), videos (mp4, mov, avi), and audio (mp3, wav, aac, and others) as reference material.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits?
faq.answer: The skill detects credit errors and shows instructions for purchasing more credits through the pexo.ai website.

faq.question: Can I make changes to a video after it is created?
faq.answer: Yes. Send revision feedback and the skill will submit it to the same project. The updated video is delivered after processing.
